title: astragalus incanus subsp. macrorhizus
intro: Astragalus incanus subsp. macrorhizus, also known as Astragalus, is a plant species within the legume family, characterized by its long taproot and its use in traditional Chinese medicine. The medicinal properties of Astragalus incanus subsp. macrorhizus include its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory effects, which enable it to boost the immune system and help the body adapt to stress. The health benefits associated with Astragalus incanus subsp. macrorhizus include reducing fatigue, improving immune function, and enhancing respiratory health, making it a popular herbal remedy for common colds and flu. Astragalus incanus subsp. macrorhizus can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as capsules, tea, and tinctures, allowing for a range of dosing options to suit individual needs.
